title: Career Blueprint
prerequisites: All previous beginner missions completed or in progress
learning_objectives: Perform honest self-assessment of acquired skills
Curate portfolio artifacts from completed missions
scenario: You have completed Beginner Offensive. Before moving to Intermediate, document where you have been, where you are, and where you are going. This becomes a key portfolio piece and your roadmap for the next 12 months.

scenario_context.setting: End of Beginner track reflection and planning
scenario_context.challenge: Honest self-assessment and strategic career planning
scenario_context.relevance: Self-awareness is what separates professionals who grow from those who plateau. Your career blueprint is a living document.

subtasks.title: Skills Inventory
subtasks.description: List every skill gained through the Beginner track. Rate yourself 1-5 on each. Be honest.
subtasks.detailed_guidance: Use the full curriculum as a checklist. Rate: 1=Heard of it, 2=Understand concept, 3=Can do with guidance, 4=Can do independently, 5=Can teach others. Be brutally honest.

subtasks.title: Portfolio Review
subtasks.description: Gather your best 3 mission outputs. Write a one-paragraph summary of each explaining what you did and learned.
subtasks.detailed_guidance: Choose 3 missions where you're most proud of your work. For each: what was the challenge, what did you do, what did you learn, what would you do differently?

subtasks.title: 12-Month Roadmap
subtasks.description: Create a month-by-month plan: OCH progression, certifications, CTF participation, community involvement, portfolio milestones.
subtasks.detailed_guidance: Month 1-2: OCH Intermediate. Month 3: CTF competitions. Month 4: eJPT prep. Month 5: HackTheBox/VulnHub. Month 6: Portfolio building. Adjust to your reality. Be specific.

subtasks.title: Identity Statement
subtasks.description: Write your Offensive Security Professional Identity Statement: who you are becoming, what you stand for, and how you will use your skills ethically.
subtasks.detailed_guidance: One page. Who are you becoming professionally? What ethical principles guide your work? How will you contribute to African cybersecurity? This is personal and professional.

reflection_prompt: In 12 months, you want to be employed in cybersecurity. What is the single most important thing you need to do in the next 30 days to make that happen?

quiz.question: Honest self-assessment is valuable because:
options: It looks good on a resume
It identifies specific gaps you can target for improvement
quiz.explanation: Knowing your actual skill level prevents overconfidence and enables targeted improvement where it matters most.

quiz.question: A professional identity statement is important because:
options: It clarifies your values and direction, guiding decisions throughout your career
quiz.explanation: A clear professional identity helps you make career decisions, maintain ethical boundaries, and communicate your value to employers.

mentor_notes: Honesty in self-assessment is more important than high ratings. The identity statement reveals professional maturity. The roadmap should be realistic and specific to their situation.
